event_comments:
2018-WES #1 – Lake Wilderness
Thank You All Volunteers!

The second event in the 2018 Wednesday Evening Series will be held at North Seattle College. The Wednesday Evening Series is our most relaxed, casual set of navigation events, held in local parks once the weather warms up. These events are a little more low-key than those in the WIOL/Winter Series, and we always offer a barbeque afterward for all participants. Decompress after work, with or without your coworkers. Or meet your family at the park and enjoy a night off from cooking. Or meet a bunch of friends at the park for an evening of exploring together, chatting over some food, and finishing up with frisbee on the lawn.
Events in the Wednesday Evening Series are generally less challenging (both physically and navigationally) than many of our other events, and are therefore great for beginners and those less experienced with orienteering – while also offering a challenge to longtime participants of the sport.
North Seattle College is an unique orienteering venue which includes an array of park land around the central urban campus. The main challenge here lies in the fact that the campus complex is multi-leveled, which means that you’ll often need to decide whether to go up, down, or stay on your current level to reach your control. Luckily, the map was created with a set of special symbols designed to help you do just that! See the North Seattle College map for an explanation of these symbols, or ask someone at the event – we’ll be happy to help!
One safety note – the map also includes Licton Springs Park, which is located a couple blocks west of the College and will be visited by those who undertake Course 3 or Course 4. There will be two legal crossings of College Way indicated on your map – please cross at these designated crossings, and please be careful when crossing!
